factors.summary: Free cash flow generation improved, with net cash inflows from operating activities increasing by $59 million year-over-year.

evidence.text: The $59.0 million increase in cash flows from operating activities during the nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the corresponding period last year was due primarily to a $232.0 million increase in cash generated from changes within our working capital accounts (discussed below) off-set in part by a $107.0 million decrease in Group net earnings.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow increased, indicating strong cash generation.

factors.summary: The company made small acquisitions, contributing to cash usage for investing activities.

evidence.text: We used $92.8 million of cash and cash equivalents for investing activities during the nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to $63.7 million used during the corresponding period last year. The $29.0 million increase in cash used is due to two small acquisitions in fiscal 2016, partially offset by lower additions of property and equipment in fiscal 2016 as compared to the prior fiscal year.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company made small acquisitions, but the impact on cash usage is moderate.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements negatively impacted backlog, particularly in the Building & Infrastructure LOB, with a $160.6 million decrease due to currency exchange rate changes.

evidence.text: Backlog at July 1, 2016 included a decrease of $160.6 million due to changes in currency exchange rates.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements caused a significant decrease in backlog.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards lower-margin segments, particularly in the Industrial segment, which has seen an increase in new Life Sciences projects offset by a decline in Mining & Minerals due to weak market conditions.

evidence.text: The increase in revenues for both the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016, as compared to the prior year comparable periods, was primarily due to new Life Sciences projects offset by a decline in the Mining & Minerals business due to weak market conditions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix shift towards lower-margin segments and decline in certain businesses.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining in several segments, particularly in the Petroleum & Chemicals and Buildings & Infrastructure segments, driven by reduced client spending.

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable periods was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Declining unit volumes in key segments due to reduced client spending.

factors.summary: Labor costs are being managed through restructuring and cost reduction efforts, which have delivered measurable savings.

evidence.text: The decrease for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year was due primarily to higher SG&A savings associated with the 2015 Restructuring.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are being effectively managed, leading to savings.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to restructuring efforts and operational efficiencies in several segments.

evidence.text: Proactive cost control and restructuring efforts contributed to the increase in operating profit for both the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable periods.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements from restructuring and operational efficiencies.

factors.summary: Debt levels are decreasing, with net interest expense lower in the current period due to refinancing.

evidence.text: Net interest expense for the nine months ended July 1, 2016 was $5.2 million as compared to $9.8 million for the corresponding period last year. Included in net interest expense for the nine months ended July 1, 2016 was a reversal of $2.7 million of accrued interest expense related to the statute expiration of a foreign tax reserve.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Debt levels are decreasing, improving financial health.

factors.summary: The company is maintaining shareholder returns through share repurchases, with a focus on capital preservation.

evidence.text: During the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016, the Company repurchased and retired 0.6 million and 2.4 million shares, respectively, of its common stock under its July 23, 2015 share repurchase program, which was first utilized in the first quarter of fiscal 2016.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively repurchasing shares, indicating confidence in its value and returning value to shareholders.

factors.summary: Management appears cautious, with actions such as share repurchases and cost reduction efforts indicating confidence in managing through current challenges.

evidence.text: During the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016, the Company repurchased and retired 0.6 million and 2.4 million shares, respectively, of its common stock under its July 23, 2015 share repurchase program, which was first utilized in the first quarter of fiscal 2016.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management's actions, including share repurchases and cost reductions, suggest confidence in navigating current challenges.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating across the company, particularly affecting the Petroleum & Chemicals and Buildings & Infrastructure segments which have seen revenue decreases. New orders are insufficient to offset cancellations and scope reductions, contributing to the overall decline.

evidence.text: Although backlog at July 1, 2016 includes new awards from clients operating in many of the Company’s LOBs, these awards were insufficient to off-set the effects of work-off, project cancellations, and reductions in scope.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Total revenues for the third quarter of fiscal 2016 decreased by $213.7 million, or 7.3%, to $2.69 billion from $2.91 billion for the third quarter of fiscal 2015.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Revenue growth is decelerating, with declines in key segments, indicating a negative trend.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are negatively impacting the company across multiple sectors, primarily due to low commodity prices and reduced demand. The Petroleum & Chemicals sector is particularly affected by declining oil prices, which have reduced clients' capital expenditures and slowed new contract awards. Additionally, the Industrial segment faces challenges from reduced commodity prices affecting its backlog.

evidence.text: Reduced commodity prices (e.g., copper and iron ore) are still affecting backlog of our Industrial LOB.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: The decrease in revenues for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable periods was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America, as the decline in oil prices has continued to negatively impact clients’ capital expenditures spending, and the pace of new contract awards.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Negative economic conditions, such as low commodity prices, are impacting multiple sectors and reducing demand.

factors.summary: The backlog orders decreased by $490.5 million, or 2.6%, to $18.3 billion, primarily due to project cancellations, scope reductions, and work-off exceeding sales. Although new awards were received, they were insufficient to offset these reductions.

evidence.text: Our backlog decreased $490.5 million, or 2.6%, to $18.3 billion at July 1, 2016 from $18.8 billion at June 26, 2015. Of the $490.5 million decrease, approximately $353.5 million is due to the effects of foreign exchange. Although backlog at July 1, 2016 includes new awards from clients operating in many of the Company’s LOBs, these awards were insufficient to off-set the effects of work-off, project cancellations, and reductions in scope.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

evidence.text: Backlog at July 1, 2016 was $18.3 billion, and represents a decrease of $490.5 million over backlog at June 26, 2015.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Decrease in backlog orders due to cancellations and scope reductions, despite new awards.

factors.summary: Demand trends are mixed across different sectors. While the Aerospace & Technology and Buildings & Infrastructure segments experienced higher operating profits, other sectors like Petroleum & Chemicals, Industrial, and Mining & Minerals are facing pressure due to lower commodity prices and reduced capital spending. Overall, demand is decelerating in several key segments, particularly in the Middle East and North America, impacting clients' capital expenditures and new contract awards.

evidence.text: The effects of lower prices of crude oil and certain commodities, including copper and iron ore, continue to put pressure on the capital spending plans of our clients operating in the Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial LOBs, principally in the mining and minerals business. However, we experienced higher operating profit during the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 in the Aerospace & Technology and Buildings & Infrastructure LOBs as compared to the corresponding period last year.

The decrease in revenues for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable periods was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America, as the decline in oil prices has continued to negatively impact clients’ capital expenditures spending, and the pace of new contract awards.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Mixed demand with significant declines in key sectors due to economic factors.

factors.summary: The Aerospace & Technology and Buildings & Infrastructure segments are experiencing growth, while the Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial segments are facing slower demand and growth declines.

evidence.text: The Aerospace & Technology and Buildings & Infrastructure LOBs are growing faster compared to the previous year, while Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial LOBs face slower demand.
Aerospace & Technology revenues for the three months ended July 1, 2016 decreased $31.3 million, or 4.5%, to $667.8 million from $699.1 million for the corresponding period last year.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Growth in some segments but decline in others, leading to mixed signals.

factors.summary: The company is experiencing mixed margin performance. While margins are under pressure due to lower prices and capital spending, cost reduction efforts have partially offset these challenges, leading to stable or slightly improving gross and operating margins in certain segments.

evidence.text: Actions taken pursuant to the Company’s 2015 Restructuring have lowered the Company’s overall cost structure, which is helping to offset negative pressures we are experiencing in certain of our LOBs.

As a percentage of revenues, direct costs of contracts for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 was 83.2% and 83.9%, respectively. This compares to 83.3% and 83.4% for the three and nine months ended June 26, 2015, respectively.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Mixed margin performance with some improvement but overall pressure remains.

factors.summary: Restructuring and cost reduction actions, including workforce reductions, office consolidations, and share repurchases, have delivered measurable savings and are on track.

evidence.text: The 2015 Restructuring was not completed in fiscal 2015, and actions related to the 2015 Restructuring continued into fiscal 2016. Actions related to the 2015 Restructuring completed during the third quarter of fiscal 2016 include involuntary terminations, the abandonment of certain leased offices, combining operational organizations, and the co-location of employees into other existing offices.

During the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016, the Company repurchased and retired 0.6 million and 2.4 million shares, respectively, of its common stock under its July 23, 2015 share repurchase program, which was first utilized in the first quarter of fiscal 2016.
evidence.subsection: JACOBS ENGINEERING GROUP INC. AND SUBSIDIARIES

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Restructuring actions have delivered measurable savings and are on track.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include reduced client spending in certain sectors, particularly in the Middle East and North America, due to lower commodity prices affecting capital spending plans. Geopolitical factors also impact contract awards in these regions.

evidence.text: The effects of lower prices of crude oil and certain commodities, including copper and iron ore, continue to put pressure on the capital spending plans of our clients operating in the Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial LOBs, principally in the mining and minerals business.

The decrease in revenues for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable periods was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America, as the decline in oil prices has continued to negatively impact clients’ capital expenditures spending, and the pace of new contract awards.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include reduced client spending due to lower commodity prices and geopolitical factors impacting contract awards.

factors.summary: The company faces significant sensitivity to commodity prices, particularly oil prices, which negatively impact client capital spending in the Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial segments. Additionally, changes in government contract preferences pose risks to the Aerospace & Technology segment.

evidence.text: The effects of lower prices of crude oil and certain commodities...putting pressure on the capital spending plans of our clients operating in the Petroleum & Chemicals and Industrial LOBs, principally in the mining and minerals business.

The decrease in revenues for the three and nine months ended July 1, 2016 as compared to the prior year comparable periods was primarily due to lower business volume in the Oil & Gas market sector and to a lesser extent the Refining market sector, particularly in the Middle East and North America, as the decline in oil prices has continued to negatively impact clients’ capital expenditures spending, and the pace of new contract awards.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Significant sensitivity to commodity prices, particularly oil, negatively impacts client capital spending and government contract preferences pose risks.
